name: Jul-Gonu
author: Traditional (Korea) / AGP port
description: Jul-Gonu (줄고누, 'line gonu'), a traditional Korean custodial-capture game on a 4x4 board. Four pieces each start on the players' back ranks; a piece steps one point orthogonally, and no move may recreate any earlier position. Capture is active custodial: after your move, one or two enemy pieces sandwiched in an unbroken row/column line between the moved piece and another friendly piece are removed (moving into a sandwich is safe; a row and a column capture can both fire at once). Win by reducing the opponent to a single piece or stalemating them. Exhaustively analysed: without the repetition ban the game is a draw — the anti-repetition rule is what forces a decision.
